evidence.symptom_phrase: "Allow getParameter to be executed locally against an arbiter in an authenticated replica set"

timeline.text:
"Allow getParameter to be executed locally against an arbiter in an authenticated replica set"

"""{panel:title=Issue Status as of Apr 29, 2015|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#6CB33F|bgColor=#EEEEEE}    *ISSUE SUMMARY*  Because arbiters do not replicate data, they do not have a copy of a replica set's credentials, and therefore refuse all authentication attempts when {{auth}} is required. As a result certain operations required for basic maintenance and for integration with MMS, that were previousily available via the localhost exception, are no longer possible.     This change makes following operations available to clients that have access to an arbiter via the localhost exception:     - {{getCmdLineOpts}}  - {{serverStatus}}  - {{getParameter}}  - shutdown    *WORKAROUNDS*    None.    *AFFECTED VERSIONS*    3.0.0, 3.0.1, and 3.0.2    *FIX VERSION*  The fix is included in the 3.0.3 production release.    {panel}    h6. Original description    In 2.6.x it was possible to execute getParameter against an arbiter in an authenticated replica set. In 3.0.1 it is not.    Please restore the ability to execute getParameter against an arbiter in an authenticated replica set, at least from localhost."""

evidence.symptom_phrase: "Allow getParameter to be executed locally against an arbiter in an authenticated replica set"

evidence.symptom_phrase: "Refactor how WiredTigerRecoveryUnit and WiredTigerSnapshotManager begin transactions at points in time"

timeline.text:
"Refactor how WiredTigerRecoveryUnit and WiredTigerSnapshotManager begin transactions at points in time"

"""There are [4 different Timestamp member variables|https://github.com/datastore/mongo/blob/master/src/mongo/db/storage/wiredtiger/wiredtiger_recovery_unit.h#L151-L169] and equally as many booleans to track how we open transactions, timestamp transactions, and commit transactions. The relationship between the recovery unit and the snapshot manager is equally as confusing.     There are 5 ways to open a transaction and 2 ways to commit.    On top of that, the {{ignore_prepare}} flag has to be set in each of the 5 places we begin transactions, and sometimes with additional configuration parameters.    My suggestions for improvement (at a minimum) are:    * Only call begin_transaction in one place, potentially as a helper on the WiredTigerSession or the RecoveryUnit itself.  * Use an enum to keep track of what type of read/commit we are doing for the open transaction.  * To reduce timestamp confusion we should only have to keep track of 2 timestamps: when to read and when to commit.  * The SnapshotManager should only keep track of timestamps (or removed completely), and not be the designated caller to open special types of transactions."""

evidence.symptom_phrase: "Refactor how WiredTigerRecoveryUnit and WiredTigerSnapshotManager begin transactions at points in time"

timeline.text: "Taking out of epic, as this is not required by the scope or design."

evidence.symptom_phrase: "Refactor how WiredTigerRecoveryUnit and WiredTigerSnapshotManager begin transactions at points in time"

timeline.text: "Author:{'email': '<EMAIL>', 'username': 'louiswilliams', 'name': '<USER>} Message: TICKET-XXXXX Refactor WiredTigerRecoveryUnit and WiredTigerSnapshotManager. An enum is now used to indicate at which time to read on a RecoveryUnit. Branch: master https://github.com/datastore/mongo/commit/6d2de545a7cfcf4ab23dcf73426a1d50896d6d0c"

evidence.symptom_phrase: "Dropping unsharded collection fails to synchronize with a concurrent shardCollection"

timeline.text:
"Dropping unsharded collection fails to synchronize with a concurrent shardCollection"

"""Currently the sharded collection drop code path branches for sharded or unsharded collections when in practice there is no reason why it should not be the same for both.    This causes race conditions between dropping an unsharded collection and sharding the same collection concurrently, because dropping the unsharded collection will not take the distributed lock.    Making both of them go through the catalog client also helps with moving that command to the config server primary."""

evidence.symptom_phrase: "Dropping unsharded collection fails to synchronize with a concurrent shardCollection"

timeline.text: "Author:{u'username': u'kaloianm', u'name': u'<USER>, u'email': u'<EMAIL>'} Message: TICKET-XXXXX Get rid of ShardingCatalogClient::updateCollection Branch: master https://github.com/datastore/mongo/commit/a301a8450b394060700011135c071db29309cd4c"

evidence.symptom_phrase: "Dropping unsharded collection fails to synchronize with a concurrent shardCollection"

evidence.symptom_phrase: Prometheus rule evaluation lagging during cardinality spike

timeline.text:
Prometheus rule evaluation lagging during high cardinality spikes.

Customer mentioned experiencing delays in Prometheus rule evaluations when there's a sudden increase in data cardinality. This is impacting their monitoring and alerting systems, causing some alerts to be delayed. The issue seems to affect the prometheus-eval component specifically. Urgency is marked as medium as it's disrupting operations but not critically so at this time. Could you please investigate this further?

Can you look into this?

evidence.symptom_phrase: Prometheus rule evaluation lagging during cardinality spike

timeline.text: Received. Checking `prometheus-eval` dashboard for rule evaluation latency spikes during high cardinality. Monitoring query execution times & resource usage. Will update on findings.

evidence.symptom_phrase: Prometheus rule evaluation lagging during cardinality spike

timeline.text: Increased Prometheus scrape interval and adjusted rule evaluation concurrency settings to handle high cardinality spikes more effectively. Monitoring ongoing performance.

evidence.symptom_phrase: "datastore daemon able to start w/ any valid form certificate w/o validate it against the CA"

timeline.text:
"datastore daemon able to start w/ any valid form certificate w/o validate it against the CA"

"""datastore daemon 3.0 GA    Environment:     1) Single datastore daemon  2) Certificates from an external CA authority (windows 2008), certificates attached   - newcert4.pem - Certificate and private key  - CA4.pem - CA auth certificate     Problem:    Start datastore daemon with netcert4.pem works correctly (see command line), but I expected it to work only with --sslCAFile  (AS this is not a self-sign certificate)    %datastore daemon%\datastore daemon --replSet EitanRs3a --port 5002 --dbpath d:\data\db1  --storageEngine wiredTiger -  -logpath d:\data\rs3primary.txt   --sslMode requireSSL --sslPEMKeyFile newcert4.pem """

evidence.symptom_phrase: "datastore daemon able to start w/ any valid form certificate w/o validate it against the CA"

timeline.text: Closing as resolved.

evidence.symptom_phrase: checkout order-id collision under concurrent submission burst

timeline.text:
Order ID collision during concurrent submissions in checkout service.

Customer reported an issue where multiple orders received the same order ID when submitted simultaneously, leading to potential processing errors and confusion. This occurred under high volume submission bursts. Urgency is high due to the risk of financial discrepancies and customer dissatisfaction. Please investigate and resolve this issue promptly.

evidence.symptom_phrase: checkout order-id collision under concurrent submission burst

timeline.text: acknowledged. investigating order-id collision on checkoutservice during concurrent submissions. checking dashboard for recent id generation metrics & logs. will update shortly.

evidence.symptom_phrase: checkout order-id collision under concurrent submission burst

timeline.text: Implemented a distributed lock mechanism to ensure unique order ID generation during concurrent submissions in the checkout service, preventing collisions under high volume bursts. Rolled out changes and monitoring closely.
